content:
type: SURVEY
files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Adding validate_nesting param to Context.__init__ (new attr + child tracking list), modifying Context._make_sub_context() (inherit validate_nesting, register child), Context.close() (validate no open children, raise RuntimeError, remove self from parent tracking), and Context.__exit__ (trigger validation). In shell_completion.py: _resolve_context() and its cli.make_context()/cmd.make_context() calls need validate_nesting=False passed explicitly.

type: DRAFT
files: src/click/shell_completion.py
region: _resolve_context() function
code: see note
note: Found a bug in your draft: in the non-chain branch, 'args = ctx._protected_args + ctx.args' runs BEFORE 'ctx = sub_ctx', so it uses the stale parent ctx instead of sub_ctx (original code assigned ctx=new_ctx first, then computed args from it). Fix: compute args from sub_ctx directly, e.g. 'args = sub_ctx._protected_args + sub_ctx.args' then 'ctx = sub_ctx'. Sending corrected full code next.

content:
type: AGREE
files: src/click/shell_completion.py
region: _resolve_context() function
code: def _resolve_context(
    cli: Command,
    ctx_args: cabc.MutableMapping[str, t.Any],
    prog_name: str,
    args: list[str],
) -> Context:
    """Produce the context hierarchy starting with the command and
    traversing the complete arguments. This only follows the commands,
    it doesn't trigger input prompts or callbacks.

    :param cli: Command being called.
    :param prog_name: Name of the executable in the shell.
    :param args: List of complete args before the incomplete value.
    """
    ctx_args["resilient_parsing"] = True
    with cli.make_context(
        prog_name, args.copy(), validate_nesting=False, **ctx_args
    ) as ctx:
        args = ctx._protected_args + ctx.args

        while args:
            command = ctx.command

            if isinstance(command, Group):
                if not command.chain:
                    name, cmd, args = command.resolve_command(ctx, args)

                    if cmd is None:
                        return ctx

                    with cmd.make_context(
                        name,
                        args,
                        parent=ctx,
                        resilient_parsing=True,
                        validate_nesting=False,
                    ) as sub_ctx:
                        ctx = sub_ctx
                        args = ctx._protected_args + ctx.args
                else:
                    sub_ctx = ctx

                    while args:
                        name, cmd, args = command.resolve_command(ctx, args)

                        if cmd is None:
                            return ctx

                        with cmd.make_context(
                            name,
                            args,
                            parent=ctx,
                            allow_extra_args=True,
                            allow_interspersed_args=False,
                            resilient_parsing=True,
                            validate_nesting=False,
                        ) as sub_sub_ctx:
                            sub_ctx = sub_sub_ctx
                            args = sub_ctx.args

                    ctx = sub_ctx
                    args = [*sub_ctx._protected_args, *sub_ctx.args]
            else:
                break

    return ctx
note: Confirmed identical, will write verbatim. This depends on Context.__init__ accepting a validate_nesting kwarg (my construct in core.py) - proceeding to draft that now.

fields.code:
def _resolve_context(
    cli: Command,
    ctx_args: cabc.MutableMapping[str, t.Any],
    prog_name: str,
    args: list[str],
) -> Context:
    """Produce the context hierarchy starting with the command and
    traversing the complete arguments. This only follows the commands,
    it doesn't trigger input prompts or callbacks.

    :param cli: Command being called.
    :param prog_name: Name of the executable in the shell.
    :param args: List of complete args before the incomplete value.
    """
    ctx_args["resilient_parsing"] = True
    with cli.make_context(
        prog_name, args.copy(), validate_nesting=False, **ctx_args
    ) as ctx:
        args = ctx._protected_args + ctx.args

        while args:
            command = ctx.command

            if isinstance(command, Group):
                if not command.chain:
                    name, cmd, args = command.resolve_command(ctx, args)

                    if cmd is None:
                        return ctx

                    with cmd.make_context(
                        name,
                        args,
                        parent=ctx,
                        resilient_parsing=True,
                        validate_nesting=False,
                    ) as sub_ctx:
                        ctx = sub_ctx
                        args = ctx._protected_args + ctx.args
                else:
                    sub_ctx = ctx

                    while args:
                        name, cmd, args = command.resolve_command(ctx, args)

                        if cmd is None:
                            return ctx

                        with cmd.make_context(
                            name,
                            args,
                            parent=ctx,
                            allow_extra_args=True,
                            allow_interspersed_args=False,
                            resilient_parsing=True,
                            validate_nesting=False,
                        ) as sub_sub_ctx:
                            sub_ctx = sub_sub_ctx
                            args = sub_ctx.args

                    ctx = sub_ctx
                    args = [*sub_ctx._protected_args, *sub_ctx.args]
            else:
                break

    return ctx

type: DONE
files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
note: validate_nesting kwarg is now live on Context.__init__ (default False), tracked via self._children, inherited in _make_sub_context(), and enforced in close() with RuntimeError. Verified end-to-end: shell completion (group/subcommand/chained) still works, and validate_nesting=True correctly raises when a parent closes with an open child, and succeeds once child is closed first. Full test suite passes. shell_completion.py written with the agreed _resolve_context() verbatim.
